etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 厄(厄) (è) -> uh...
etymology.interpretation: The character 呃(呃) (è, "uh...") combines the semantic element 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating sounds made with the mouth, with the phonetic element 厄(厄) (è), suggesting the sound "è". It represents a hesitation sound, commonly used to express thought or minor discomfort verbally.

examples.chinese: 呃(呃)，我不太确定怎么回答这个问题(我不太確定怎麼回答這個問題)。
examples.pinyin: È, wǒ bù tài quèdìng zěnme huídá zhège wèntí.
examples.english: Uh, I'm not quite sure how to answer this question.

examples.chinese: 我正在写信(我正在寫信)，但是在思考下一句该怎么说(但是在思考下一句該怎麼說)时，呃(呃)，停住了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài xiě xìn, dànshì zài sīkǎo xià yī jù gāi zěnme shuō shí, è, tíng zhù le.
examples.english: I was writing a letter, but while thinking about what to say next, uh, I paused.

examples.chinese: 他问我(他問我)今晚有没有安排(今晚有沒有安排)，我说呃(我說呃)，还没有想好(還沒有想好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wèn wǒ jīnwǎn yǒu méiyǒu ānpái, wǒ shuō è, hái méiyǒu xiǎng hǎo.
examples.english: He asked me if I had plans for tonight, and I said uh, I haven't decided yet.
